narration: Level order uses BFS, so the root is the whole first row by itself.
callout: The first queue row is just [3].

narration: The next queue row becomes [9, 20], which is the second tree level.
decision: Queue size tells how many nodes belong to the current level.

narration: Then [15, 7] becomes the third level. BFS keeps rows separate by processing one queue-length chunk at a time.
